content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 9, 2016

Vera M. I. Todorov
Senior Deputy City Attorney
200 East Santa Clara Street, 16th Floor Tower
San Jose, CA 95113-1905

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-16-014

Dear Ms. Todorov:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of City of San Jose Planning
Commissioner Michelle Yesney regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act"). Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest
provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common
law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

QUESTIONS

1. May Commissioner Yesney participate in Planning Commission decisions involving
Powers & Associate, Inc. where approximately three years ago she was employed and was a 20%
owner?

2. May Commissioner Yesney participate in Planning Commission decisions involving
environmental documents she worked on while employed at Powers & Associates, including an
Environmental Impact Report, for the city's General Plan?

CONCLUSIONS

1. & 2. Yes. Commissioner Yesney does not have a financial interest in these decisions.

FACTS

Commissioner Yesney was appointed to the San Jose Planning Commission on October 6,
2015 to fill a vacancy and could be reappointed by the City Council.

On December 20, 2012, Commissioner Yesney retired from her employment with Powers
and Associate, Inc. ("Powers"), a closely held corporation where she served as Vice President and
shareholder. Commissioner Yesney owned 20 percent of Powers' outstanding shares prior to her
retirement.

File No. A-16-014
Page No. 2

One year before her retirement, Commissioner Yesney entered into written agreements to
sell all of her shares in the firm upon her retirement to three employees, Martha Silveira, Chief
Financial Officer, John Schwarz, Vice President/Principal Project Manager, and Akoni Danielsen,
Principal Project Manager. Each purchase agreement provided for a down payment and equal
annual installments. In December 2015, Ms. Silveira and Mr. Swarz paid off their obligations in
full. Mr. Danielsen still owes Commissioner Yesney $18,128.60 which is due in December 2016.

While employed at Powers, Commissioner Yesney worked on environmental documents
required by the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) that the firm provided as a
consultant for the city and for private developers or property owners. Under CEQA, documents that
consultants prepare must be independently reviewed for adequacy and approved by the city council.

Mr. Danielsen, as an employee of Powers, has and may in the future, work on CEQA
documents for the city or developers. Ms. Silveira does not work on CEQA documents.
Mr. Schwarz will not be working on such documents in the future because he no longer works at or
owns any stock in Powers.

While employed at Powers, Commissioner Yesney supervised and contributed to the
preparation of some environmental impact reports (EIRs) and other CEQA documents for the City,
including the EIR for the City's 2040 General Plan adopted in 2011. The preparation of the Final
EIR, as with all CEQA documents, was supervised by City staff and the final document was
certified as complete and in conformance with CEQA by both the Planning Commission and the
City Council. The EIR for the 2040 General Plan is used frequently as the basis of environmental
review for subsequent projects that go before the Planning Commission for approval or
recommendation to the City Council.

Since Commissioner Yesney's retirement, Powers continues to prepare environmental
documents for projects that come before the Planning Commission for approval or
recommendation. Powers employees who work on the environmental documents are paid regardless
of whether the projects are approved or denied. Generally the firm is paid in full before the project
for which the document was prepared goes to hearing before the Planning Commission or the City
Council. Once a project is approved or denied, Powers has no further involvement in the project.

The Planning Commission has continued to rely on environmental documents prepared by
Powers employees in considering applications, included documents Commissioner Yesney worked
on. Her work related primarily to the EIR for the City's General Plan.

You have asked us to limit our analysis to the above questions. You have determined that
Commissioner Yesney is precluded from participating in decisions involving Mr. Danielsen,
Mr. Schwarz and Ms. Silveira personally until 12 months after they have paid in full for their stock.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the

File No. A-16-014
Page No. 3

official has a financial interest. The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions only apply to conflicts of
interests based on the following:

• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).)

• Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000
or more. (Section 87103(b).)

• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)

• Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).)

• Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $460 or
more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e).)

• In addition, a public official's personal finances are deemed to be directly involved in a
governmental decision that will have any financial effect on his or her personal finances or
those of his or her immediate family. (Section 87103.)

Decisions Involving Powers

As stated above, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in
which the official has a financial interest. You state that Commissioner Yesney has received no
compensation from Powers since leaving in December 2012 (over three years ago). Therefore,
Powers is not a source of income to her. Also, since she no longer holds any position with Powers
or own any Powers stock, she does not have a business entity financial interest in Powers.
Therefore, Commissioner Yesney does not have a financial interest in Powers and is not precluded
from participating in Planning Commission decisions involving Powers.

Decisions Related to CEQA Documents Commissioner Yesney Prepared

As with decisions involving Powers, Commissioner Yesney does not have a financial
interest in these decisions. Accordingly, the Act's conflict of interest provisions do not prohibit her
from participating in these decisions.

File No. A-16-014
Page No. 4

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division

VJ:jgl
